subject: R6 updates for dcaegen2/dcaemod
commit_message:
R6 updates for dcaegen2/dcaemod

Update to bootstrap container 1.12.2
Update to dashboard container 1.3.2
Update to prh 1.5.2
Make runtime-api imports configurable

Issue-ID: CCSDK-2158
Issue-ID: DCAEGEN2-2088
Issue-ID: DCAEGEN2-2105
Issue-ID: DCAEGEN2-2120
Issue-ID: DCAEGEN2-2127
Issue-ID: DCAEGEN2-2128
Signed-off-by: Jack Lucas <jflucas@research.att.com>
Change-Id: Ie335fc866bc5360bbfc8200fc0e1712b64e05e1b

entries.message: Uploaded patch set 1.

Patch Set 1:

> Patch Set 1:
> 
> @Jack @Vijay
> does any of you know the reason why onap-sdc-dcae-fe is failing? Logs for this pod are completely empty which doesn't help to debug it...

Don't know why this is failing.  I haven't seen this particular failure in our local environment.  Maybe @Vijay has a clue.  (SDC has been pretty stable in my environment recently--some of the SDC initialization jobs fail at first and run multiple times, but eventually they succeed.)

Patch Set 1:

> Patch Set 1:

Sorry no clue on SDC failure, possibly Ofir can check and advise if this is seen across on other OOM gating.  But there is nothing in this patch that relates to SDC itself.

This is an interesting and puzzling result.   The bootstrap container's readiness initcontainer waits for dcae-cloudify-manager to become ready, so dcae-cloudify-manager must have been ready at some point.  From the bootstrap init container log:

2020-03-18 21:39:56,017 - INFO - Checking if dcae-cloudify-manager  is ready
2020-03-18 21:39:56,690 - INFO - dcae-cloudify-manager is not ready.
2020-03-18 21:40:01,701 - INFO - Checking if dcae-cloudify-manager  is ready
2020-03-18 21:40:02,414 - INFO - dcae-cloudify-manager is not ready.
2020-03-18 21:40:07,425 - INFO - Checking if dcae-cloudify-manager  is ready
2020-03-18 21:40:08,086 - INFO - dcae-cloudify-manager is ready!

Also, bootstrap deploys some dcae components using dcae-cloudify-manager, and we can see that they were in fact deployed.  (The deployments labeled with the "dep-" prefix were created by dcae-cloudify-manager on request from bootstrap.)  The bootstrap log is truncated, but the log information that's present shows that the bootstrap container was making successful requests to dcae-cloudify-manager.

dcae-cloudify-manager stops logging to stdout/stderr early in its startup, so it's not possible to tell what happened.  But clearly it ran long enough to do the initial deployment work and then it became unready.   There are additional logs on the container file system that might provide clues. 

In short, this looks to be a post-installation problem.  If something similar happened in one of my environments, I would exec into the dcae-cloudify-manager container (it's still running) and investigate.
